text: Statistical heterogeneity is a crucial challenge in distributed learning scenarios like FL, where the global average model does not learn effective representations for local data distributions and therefore fails to generalize well to heterogeneous local datasets across clients. To overcome this challenge, we propose a new personalized framework for FL, named as - FedALA. FedALA leverages the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module, which acts as a personalization mechanism at the client-side that fuses the global average model received from the server and its local model based on the objective pertinent to its unique distribution. ALA is applied during the initialization stage of the local training loop, and as a result, the starting point for the local training iterations is always optimized to the local task and data distribution, which helps achieve the global objective of FL through each local update. In our experiments, we demonstrate that FedALA yields superior performance across five datasets sampled from diverse computer vision and natural language processing tasks. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the ALA module adopted as a plug-in replacement for other well-known personalized and non-personalized FL algorithms can lead to better test accuracy (reaching up to 3.27% on average) than all eleven comparison points. In addition, the ALA module can increase the accuracy of other personalized FL methods by as much as 24.19%. Source code for implementing the proposed FedALA is available.
